figures.caption: Table 3: Sample quality metrics across datasets and architectures. FID is computed against real test images (1,000 for MNIST; 10,000 for CIFAR-10).
Table 1: Watermark detection performance across all models and datasets.

Figure 1: Overview of the proposed dynamics-level watermarking framework. Top (Training): A message m and secret key K define a time-modulated watermark signal sK,m, which is added to the velocity field during flow matching. Bottom (Detection): Given black-box access to a model, the detector projects velocity queries into the code space, demodulates with the temporal carrier, and correlates against the codebook to recover the embedded message. The same key K is used for embedding and detection.
Table 6: Complete hyperparameter configuration for all experiments.
Figure 2: Generated MNIST samples from UNet + LoRA models. Top row: real MNIST digits. Second row: clean UNet model. Rows 3–5: watermarked LoRA models.
Figure 3: Generated CIFAR-10 samples from UNet + LoRA models. Top row: real CIFAR-10 images. Second row: clean UNet model. Rows 3–5: watermarked LoRA models.
Table 4: Comparison with existing generative model watermarking methods.
